grid.rule: All icons in a product must use a consistent grid size. Don't mix 16px and 24px icons in the same context.
grid.recommended: 20px for most UI. 16px for dense/inline contexts. 24px for action buttons. Never use 18px or 22px.
grid.optical_sizing: Filled icons appear heavier — optically reduce by 1px. Stroke icons at same size feel lighter. Match visual weight, not pixel dimensions.

stroke_vs_filled.stroke: Outline/line icons — lighter, modern, preferred for navigation, text-adjacent, labels
stroke_vs_filled.filled: Solid icons — heavier, used for active states, important alerts, and emphasis
stroke_vs_filled.rule: Pick one style for the product and use it consistently. Mix only intentionally: stroke=inactive, filled=active (tabs, sidebar nav).

stroke_weight.rule: Don't use 1px strokes — they become invisible at small sizes and in dark mode

style.corner_radius: Match icon corner radius to the product's border-radius language. Rounded product → rounded icons. Sharp product → sharp icons.
style.consistency: All icons should share: same grid, same stroke weight, same corner radius, same optical weight. One off-brand icon makes the system look amateurish.

usage_rules: Icons without labels must have aria-label or be paired with tooltip — never rely on icon meaning alone
Decorative icons (purely visual) must have aria-hidden='true' and focusable='false'
Icon + label: minimum 8px gap (gap-2). Never vertically misaligned — use flex items-center.
Interactive icon buttons: minimum 44×44px touch target. 32×32px visible icon area with invisible padding to 44px.
NEVER scale icons with font-size (em units) unless intentionally coupling them to text size
Color: inherit from parent text color by default (currentColor). Override only when semantic (danger icon = danger color).

size_map.use: Dense tables, inline text, tags, breadcrumbs
Default UI — sidebar nav, form labels, menu items
Button icons, card header actions, primary nav
Feature icons in marketing sections, onboarding illustrations
Empty state icons, hero feature icons
Large empty states, step indicators in onboarding

avoid: Custom SVGs that don't match chosen library's stroke weight

antipatterns: Using icons from multiple libraries — visual weight mismatch is immediately obvious
14px icons next to 16px body text — icon is too small to be legible
Colored icons for decoration, not meaning — if color doesn't communicate state, remove it
Interactive icon button with no visible label and no tooltip — fails accessibility
Scaling icons with CSS transform:scale() — use width/height directly for consistent rendering
